nodes.description: Enlarged polycystic liver volume causes abdominal distention.

evidence.snippet: HP:0003270 | Abdominal distention | Very frequent (99-80%)

evidence.reference_title: Polycystic liver disease: an overview of pathogenesis, clinical manifestations and management.
evidence.snippet: Major complaints include abdominal pain, abdominal distension and atypical symptoms because of voluminous cysts resulting in compression of adjacent tissue or failure of the affected organ.

nodes.description: Cystic liver enlargement and compression can cause abdominal pain.

evidence.snippet: HP:0002027 | Abdominal pain | Occasional (29-5%)

evidence.reference_title: Polycystic liver disease: an overview of pathogenesis, clinical manifestations and management.
evidence.snippet: Major complaints include abdominal pain, abdominal distension and atypical symptoms because of voluminous cysts resulting in compression of adjacent tissue or failure of the affected organ.

nodes.description: Mechanical back pain can result from massive liver enlargement.

evidence.snippet: HP:0003418 | Back pain | Occasional (29-5%)

evidence.reference_title: Outcomes and Durability of Hepatic Reduction after Combined Partial Hepatectomy and Cyst Fenestration for Massive Polycystic Liver Disease.
evidence.snippet: Cystic enlargement causes liver enlargement, which can be marked and result in dyspnea, early satiety, gastroesophageal reflux, mechanical back pain, hepatic venous outflow obstruction, portal vein and inferior vena cava compression, and, rarely, jaundice from bile duct compression.

nodes.description: Hepatic cystogenesis involves cholangiocyte hyperproliferation, abnormal fluid secretion, and altered cell-matrix interaction, generating multiple epithelial cysts throughout the liver.

evidence.reference_title: Diagnosis and management of polycystic liver disease.
evidence.snippet: Processes involved in hepatic cystogenesis include ductal plate malformation with concomitant abnormal fluid secretion, altered cell-matrix interaction and cholangiocyte hyperproliferation.

evidence.reference_title: Outcomes and Durability of Hepatic Reduction after Combined Partial Hepatectomy and Cyst Fenestration for Massive Polycystic Liver Disease.
evidence.snippet: Polycystic liver disease (PLD) is characterized by the presence of multiple cholangiocyte-derived epithelial cysts that cause progressive liver enlargement.

nodes.description: In symptomatic PLD, aspiration-sclerotherapy, cyst fenestration, and segmental hepatic resection are used selectively to decompress liver cyst burden and improve mass-effect symptoms.

evidence.reference_title: Diagnosis and management of polycystic liver disease.
evidence.snippet: Current radiological and surgical therapies for symptomatic patients include aspiration-sclerotherapy, fenestration, segmental hepatic resection and liver transplantation.

evidence.reference_title: Outcomes and Durability of Hepatic Reduction after Combined Partial Hepatectomy and Cyst Fenestration for Massive Polycystic Liver Disease.
evidence.snippet: Sustained long-term reductions in LV after PHCF can be achieved in selected patients with severe, highly symptomatic PLD.

nodes.description: Massive hepatomegaly can mechanically impair breathing, especially supine.

evidence.snippet: HP:0002094 | Dyspnea | Occasional (29-5%)

nodes.description: Liver mass effect can cause early fullness with meals.

evidence.snippet: HP:0033842 | Early satiety | Frequent (79-30%)

evidence.reference_title: Outcomes and Durability of Hepatic Reduction after Combined Partial Hepatectomy and Cyst Fenestration for Massive Polycystic Liver Disease.
evidence.snippet: Indications for surgery were abdominal distention, abdominal pain, early satiety, supine dyspnea, fatigue, uterine pro-lapse, hepatic venous outflow obstruction with ascites, obstruction of the inferior vena cava and dialysis hypotension, bile duct obstruction, and infected cysts.

nodes.description: Inefficient polycystin maturation lowers functional polycystin-1 dosage. Reduced PC1 availability is a rate-limiting cyst-promoting step in polycystic liver and kidney models.
nodes.id: Inefficient polycystin maturation and reduced PC1 dosage

evidence.reference_title: Cyst growth, polycystins, and primary cilia in autosomal dominant polycystic kidney disease.
evidence.snippet: Overexpression of Pkd1, but not Pkd2, can overcome the tendency toward cyst formation in Prkcsh and Sec63 mutants, thereby establishing PC1 as the rate-limiting component whose dosage determines both the rate and extent of cyst formation in ADPKD

nodes.description: Bile duct compression can rarely cause jaundice.

evidence.snippet: HP:0000952 | Jaundice | Occasional (29-5%)

evidence.reference_title: Outcomes and Durability of Hepatic Reduction after Combined Partial Hepatectomy and Cyst Fenestration for Massive Polycystic Liver Disease.
evidence.snippet: Cystic enlargement causes liver enlargement, which can be marked and result in dyspnea, early satiety, gastroesophageal reflux, mechanical back pain, hepatic venous outflow obstruction, portal vein and inferior vena cava compression, and, rarely, jaundice from bile duct compression.

nodes.description: Liver transplantation is reserved for severe PLD when cyst burden is not amenable to other therapy, when no liver parenchyma is spared, or when combined liver-kidney transplantation is needed.

evidence.snippet: The only curative therapy for PCLD is liver transplantation.

evidence.reference_title: Liver transplantation for massive hepatomegaly due to polycystic liver disease: an extreme case.
evidence.snippet: Despite preserved liver function, the patient's clinical condition was poor; she experienced dyspnea, advanced anorexia, abdominal pain, and severe ascites.

nodes.description: Multiple liver cysts enlarge the liver and compress neighboring structures, producing abdominal distention, early satiety, dyspnea, reflux, pain, edema, and occasional cholestatic features. Liver function commonly remains preserved despite substantial cyst burden.
nodes.id: Mass-effect hepatomegaly with preserved liver function

evidence.snippet: Though isolated PCLD and PCLD due to ADPKD are genetically distinct, they follow a similar clinical course of hepatomegaly from multiple cysts with preserved liver function.

evidence.reference_title: Polycystic liver disease: an overview of pathogenesis, clinical manifestations and management.
evidence.snippet: Major complaints include abdominal pain, abdominal distension and atypical symptoms because of voluminous cysts resulting in compression of adjacent tissue or failure of the affected organ.

nodes.description: Kidney cysts can occur in ADPLD, though kidney involvement is limited compared with ADPKD.

evidence.snippet: HP:0005562 | Multiple renal cysts | Frequent (79-30%)

evidence.reference_title: Genetic Complexity of Autosomal Dominant Polycystic Kidney and Liver Diseases.
evidence.snippet: autosomal dominant polycystic liver diseases (ADPLD), which result in significant PLD with minimal PKD.

nodes.description: PRKCSH and SEC63 encode endoplasmic-reticulum proteins involved in protein biogenesis and quality control. Disease-causing variants impair maturation of integral membrane and secreted proteins that restrain cyst formation.
nodes.id: PRKCSH and SEC63 ER protein biogenesis defects

evidence.reference_title: Cyst growth, polycystins, and primary cilia in autosomal dominant polycystic kidney disease.
evidence.snippet: The two genes responsible for isolated polycystic liver disease, PRKCSH and SEC63, are both involved in modulating protein biogenesis and quality control for integral membrane and secreted proteins in the endoplasmic reticulum.

nodes.description: Venous compression and advanced mass effect can cause lower-extremity edema.

evidence.snippet: HP:0010741 | Pedal edema | Occasional (29-5%)

nodes.description: Numerous liver cysts are the defining manifestation.

evidence.snippet: HP:0006557 | Polycystic liver disease | Very frequent (99-80%)

evidence.reference_title: Modelling polycystic liver disease progression using age-adjusted liver volumes and targeted mutational analysis.
evidence.snippet: Polycystic liver disease (PLD) manifests as numerous fluid-filled cysts scattered throughout the liver parenchyma.

nodes.description: Severe liver enlargement can compromise respiratory mechanics.

evidence.snippet: HP:0002093 | Respiratory insufficiency | Occasional (29-5%)

nodes.description: Somatostatin analogs such as octreotide or lanreotide can reduce or slow polycystic liver growth, but benefits are modest and may be short-lived.

evidence.snippet: Somatostatin analogs can slow the growth of polycystic livers but the effect is short-lived.

evidence.reference_title: Diagnosis and management of polycystic liver disease.
evidence.snippet: Somatostatin analogues are promising; several clinical trials have shown that these drugs can reduce the volume of polycystic livers.
